skill.name: visual-asset-workflow
skill.description: Create educational visual briefs using story, intent, metaphor, and pedagogy checks for image generation workflows.
skill.summary: Guides educational image prompts from lesson context to distinctive, learner-appropriate visual briefs.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell backticks, C2 keywords, and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown formatting, language proficiency labels, and non-cryptographic instructional text. The skill has limited operational risk because it asks the agent to read local lesson assets, list existing image files, write a reflection document, and optionally use Google Search grounding for factual claims.

low_findings.title: Local File Review and Reflection Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to read chapter files, list image assets, read prompt files, and write a reflection document. This is legitimate for the visual workflow, but users should expect local workspace file access. No evidence found of secret collection, destructive file changes, or data exfiltration. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The referenced lines clearly require file reads and one documentation write, but the actions are scoped to project learning assets and reflections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines clearly require file reads and one documentation write, but the actions are scoped to project learning assets and reflections.

low_findings.title: Optional Web Grounding for Factual Claims
low_findings.description: The skill tells agents to enable Google Search grounding when verifying factual claims. This can trigger network access, but it is framed as citation support and not as data transfer. No evidence found of local data being sent to external services. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The line explicitly references search grounding, but the surrounding context limits it to factual verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly references search grounding, but the surrounding context limits it to factual verification.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Backtick Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown inline code spans or fenced examples, not executable Ruby code. No evidence found of actual command execution, shell invocation, or interpolated user input. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: All locations are in SKILL.md prose or Markdown formatting, and the repository contains no script file executing those snippets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All locations are in SKILL.md prose or Markdown formatting, and the repository contains no script file executing those snippets.

low_findings.title: Static C2 Keyword Findings Are Proficiency Labels
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword matches refer to CEFR-style learner proficiency levels, not command and control behavior. The surrounding text compares A2, B1, and C2 learning complexity. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: Each location appears in pedagogical guidance about student proficiency, with no networking, beaconing, or remote-control semantics.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location appears in pedagogical guidance about student proficiency, with no networking, beaconing, or remote-control semantics.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Non-Cryptographic Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in skill metadata, Gemini version references, color values, condensation guidance, and layer labels. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic implementation. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The locations are educational prompt-writing text and visual design guidance, not executable cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are educational prompt-writing text and visual design guidance, not executable cryptographic code.

content.value_statement: Educational image prompts often produce generic diagrams that miss the lesson goal. This skill turns lesson context into focused creative briefs for learner-appropriate visuals.
actual_capabilities: Builds creative briefs with story, emotional intent, metaphor, key insight, and pedagogical reasoning.
Checks visual complexity against A2, B1, and C2 learner proficiency levels.
Validates that visuals do not assume prerequisites that students have not learned.
Reviews existing chapter images and prompt files to reduce duplicate visual patterns.
Guides token condensation for large visual batches while preserving reasoning cues.
Provides a post-generation reflection framework for improving future visual work.
limitations: It does not generate images by itself; it guides prompt and workflow quality.
It assumes a chapter-based lesson structure with index and README files.
It relies on the agent to inspect local files before planning visuals.
It names Gemini as the target image reasoning model, so adaptation may be needed.

use_cases.title: Plan Lesson Illustrations
use_cases.description: Create visual briefs that match chapter objectives, prerequisites, and learner proficiency.

use_cases.title: Improve Image Prompt Quality
use_cases.description: Replace rigid pixel instructions with story-driven prompts that guide image model reasoning.

use_cases.title: Audit Visual Consistency
use_cases.description: Check existing visual prompts for duplicate patterns, unnecessary complexity, and weak teaching value.

prompt_templates.title: Plan One Visual
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single lesson image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the visual asset workflow for one chapter visual. Read the lesson context, identify the learner level, and propose a brief plan before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Creative Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for turning a learning objective into a prompt.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a creative brief for this learning objective. Include story, emotional intent, metaphor, key insight, color semantics, and pedagogical reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Visual Batch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for several visuals in one chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this chapter for visual asset opportunities. Check prerequisites, learner complexity, duplicate patterns, and propose distinct briefs for each needed visual.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize And Reflect
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after generating a large visual set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the completed visual batch. Identify success patterns, failed prompts, guardrail gaps, and reusable improvements for the next chapter.

output_examples.input: A chapter needs a visual explaining rapid growth to intermediate learners.
output_examples.output: A concise creative brief with a growth cascade metaphor, one key insight, limited elements, and clear hierarchy guidance.

output_examples.input: A beginner lesson includes a complex architecture diagram request.
output_examples.output: A planning note flags the complexity mismatch and recommends a simpler step-by-step visual with fewer elements.

output_examples.input: A chapter already has several timeline-style images.
output_examples.output: A distinctiveness review suggests alternative structures such as a hierarchy, transformation sequence, or comparison layout.

best_practices: Read chapter context and prerequisites before proposing any visual asset.
Use story, intent, metaphor, and learning purpose instead of pixel-level specifications.
Compare new prompts with existing chapter visuals before generation.
anti_patterns: Starting image prompts before checking learner level and prerequisites.
Using fixed coordinates, font sizes, and generic infographic instructions.
Creating visuals only for variety without a clear learning objective.

faq.question: Does this skill generate image files?
faq.answer: No. It creates and reviews visual briefs that can guide an image generation tool.

faq.question: Which image model does it target?
faq.answer: The instructions mention Gemini, but the briefing method can be adapted to other models.

faq.question: Why does it read chapter files first?
faq.answer: The skill uses lesson context, prerequisites, and learner level to prevent unsuitable visuals.

faq.question: Can it help with beginner lessons?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes explicit A2 constraints for simple, fast-to-understand visuals.

faq.question: How does it prevent duplicate visuals?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to inspect existing images and prompt files before drafting new prompts.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low operational risk and no confirmed malicious behavior.
